visionIndex.description: Main title of the slide, indicating the topic 'Database Denorm'.
Course code and title for context.

An Entity-Relationship Diagram (ERD) illustrating a database schema with six interconnected tables: Album, Artist, Photo, Member, Comment, and Type. Each table lists its primary key (id) and other attributes (e.g., Title, Description, Name, Email). The diagram shows one-to-many relationships between Artist and Album, Album and Photo, Photo and Type, Photo and Member, and Photo and Comment.
visionIndex.content: Album: id, Title, Description, Views. Artist: id, Name, Shortname. Photo: id, Title, Description, UploadDate, Privacy, Views. Member: id, Name, PhoneNum, Email, Address. Comment: id, PostDate, Content. Type: id, Title. Connections between Artist-Album, Album-Photo, Photo-Type, Photo-Member, Photo-Comment.

visionIndex.description: Main title of the page, 'Galaxy Schema'.
Introductory sentence defining a Galaxy Schema.
visionIndex.content: A Galaxy Schema contains two fact table that share dimension tables between them

visionIndex.description: Heading for the list of characteristics of Galaxy Schema.

Bulleted list outlining the characteristics of a Galaxy Schema, including how dimensions are separated, how it relates to hierarchy levels, its build process, dimension size, and its use for aggregating fact tables.
visionIndex.content: The dimensions in this schema are separated into separate dimensions based on the various levels of hierarchy.For example, if geography has four levels of hierarchy like region, country, state, and city then Galaxy schema should have four dimensions.Moreover, it is possible to build this type of schema by splitting the one-star schema into more Star schemes.The dimensions are large in this schema which is needed to build based on the levels of hierarchy.This schema is helpful for aggregating fact tables for better understanding.

visionIndex.description: A diagram illustrating the structure of a Galaxy Schema, showing two central fact tables (Revenue and Product) connected to multiple shared dimension tables (Dealer, Branch Dim, Date Dim, and Product).
visionIndex.content: Visual representation of the Galaxy Schema with tables and connecting lines.
